title: Software Engineer
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The role**
 As a Software Engineer at Legora, you’ll play a key role in building the technical foundation that powers our products, designing, developing, and improving the core systems used by legal professionals around the world. You’ll work across the stack, contributing to the architecture and features that define our platform.
   

  

* This is a Stockholm-based, 5-day in-office role, we believe building together in person drives better outcomes.


**What You Will Be Doing**
* Lead the design, development, and deployment of end-to-end features for our web platform, ensuring high performance, security, and reliability.
* Take full ownership of critical parts of the codebase, balancing quality, scalability, and speed of development.
* Work with technologies such as React, Node.js, AWS, Azure, and Kubernetes, leveraging the right tools to solve complex challenges.
* Collaborate closely with product and design teams to translate requirements into elegant, robust technical solutions.
* Review code and mentor colleagues, raising the overall technical bar and fostering a culture of excellence.
* Drive continuous improvement by challenging assumptions, exploring new technologies, and pushing both yourself and the team to innovate.
* Contribute to infrastructure, integrations, and architecture discussions to ensure the platform evolves sustainably.


**Who You Are**
* An engineer with experience building and shipping modern software products.
* Comfortable working across the stack, with solid skills in backend development.
* Passionate about writing maintainable, secure code—and take pride in building things that last.
* A problem solver who enjoys thinking about architecture, data flows, and long-term sustainability.
* Familiar with cloud infrastructure, distributed systems, and integrations (experience with enterprise-scale systems is a plus, but not required).
* Proactive, curious, and motivated by working in a fast-paced, collaborative startup environment.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
**About the Company** 




  





 A fast-growing technology business accelerating the transition to a renewable energy future by transforming distributed energy resources, such as batteries, solar, and wind, into flexible, revenue-generating assets. The company’s AI-driven virtual power plant platform optimises assets in real-time to deliver maximum value to customers and the wider grid. They operate in a fast-paced environment, value ambition, and seek individuals who are driven by challenges and eager to push boundaries.
 



  





**The Role** 




 They are looking for a talented
 **Senior Back-End Developer** 
 to join their expanding technical team. This role is pivotal in scaling core applications and developing innovative new features to enhance the platform’s capabilities. You will take ownership of key architectural decisions and work with cutting-edge AI tools and methods to drive productivity and innovation.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Lead architectural decisions to ensure scalability, reliability, and maintainability.

 If your interested in the role please apply below, or drop an email direct to me at b.emery@tenthrevolution.com

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
**About Kambi**
*Kambi Group plc is a leading B2B provider of premium sports betting services to licensed gaming operators. Our services provide an end-to-end solution for operators wanting to launch a standalone Sportsbook or bolster their existing offering with an innovative sports betting product. From front-end user interface to customer intelligence, risk management and odds compiling, all built on our in-house developed software, we strive to deliver the ultimate service and solution to our partners.*
*Our vision is to create the world’s leading sports betting experiences, together with our partners.*
**About Tzeract**
 Tzeract is changing the way we bet on sports. As Kambi’s in-house startup, we've built a completely new platform from the ground up - with our own AI at the core, not just as an add-on. Today we already handle over 500 millions bets in distributed systems with a low latency, and your work will be scaled globally from day one.
   

  

 We iterate quickly, decide fast, and your calendar stays free for the work that matters. This gives you time for coffee breaks, test ideas and solve problems together. In this role, you’ll help shape both the product and parts of the architecture. It’s the combination of startup freedom with an enterprise touch - and your contributions will be visible and felt by millions of users worldwide.
   

  

**What's our current focus?** 
 Well, exploring new sports markets. For you as a developer, that means diving into the rules of the game and turning them into code - building the odds infrastructure, creating models that run in real time. It's a fun and collaborative playground with a clear goal: creating the next generation of how sports, data, and AI come together - built from scratch, with impact at its core.
   

  

**Role Overview**
 This is not your usual software engineering role. At Tzeract, you'll be the bridge between software engineers and quant engineers - ensuring the models we build are optimized and able to be deployed to our cloud infrastructure. It's therefore a strong merit to find mathematical challenges exciting, since it will play a part of your daily work.
   

  

 You'll collaborate in small teams of engineers (+20 people) with expertise in quantitative analysis, data, and AI. Together, you’ll develop systems that analyze real-time data, delivering personalized experiences to sports fans everywhere.
   

  

 Tzeract is a fast-paced startup, where scope or roadmap can shift to align with future goals. If this excites you, along with a hybrid work setup and a central office location, we’d love to get to know you better.
   

  

**What you’ll do:**
* Design, develop, and maintain scalable microservices using Python & AWS technologies.
* Implement best practices in SWE, including CI/CD, containerization, and automated testing to ensure quality and speed of deployment.
* Collaborate with software and quant engineers - as well as scientists to integrate AI models and algorithms into production systems efficiently.
* Contribute to improvements on both technical and operational aspects of our daily work.


**What we believe you have:**
* Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Physics or a related field (a PhD in Mathematics is a plus).
* Solid experience in Python and/or experience with low-level languages (C++ is a plus).
* 5+ years of software development experience or similar, with the ability to quickly adapt to new technologies.
* Experience with modern DevOps tools and practices, including Docker, Jenkins, or similar.
* Great communicator, able to own your projects and gather colleagues when needed to solve complex problems.


**Note** 
 . We will only be able to consider candidates living in Sweden for this position.
   

  

***Kambi's ongoing commitment to Diversity and Inclusion in the workplace***
*If you require any reasonable adjustment during the recruitment process, please notify your recruiter, who will assist you however they can. Diversity and inclusion is at the heart of who we are and who we aim to be. While we are proud of the positive and inclusive company culture we have created, we know we can do so much more. Kambi constantly evolves its Diversity and Inclusion strategy to ensure it becomes an even more inclusive and positive place to work, with the core management team reaffirming its commitment to delivering on employee feedback.*
***Creating an inclusive environment***
*We believe Kambi's greatest strength is the collective talent of our employees. Kambi is committed to ensuring we create an inclusive work environment where everyone can feel valued, thrive and achieve their potential, regardless of who they are or what their background is. We know that it is only by having a balance of different voices, values and opinions that Kambi is able to be the market leader it is today.*
*#wearekambi*

title: BI Developer
description:
**About WPP Media**
 WPP is the creative transformation company. We use the power of creativity to build better futures for our people, planet, clients and communities. For more information, visit wpp.com.
   

  

 WPP Media is WPP’s global media collective. In a world where media is everywhere and in everything, we bring the best platform, people, and partners together to create limitless opportunities for growth. For more information, visit wppmedia.com.
   

  

**Role**
 At WPP Media, we believe in the power of our culture and our people. It’s what elevates us to deliver exceptional experiences for both our clients and each other. In this role it will be critical to embrace WPP & WPP Media’s shared core values:
   

  

* Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity.
* Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust.
* Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise.


**Role Summary And Impact**
 As a BI Developer at WPP Media, you will build and maintain Power BI solutions that help our specialists and clients follow up on media performance. The role is centered around developing user-friendly dashboards, establishing robust data streams, and creating well-structured data models that enable reliable reporting at scale. You’ll translate business needs into technical BI solutions and work closely with engineers, Data Scientists, client teams, and customers - often integrating outputs from different measurement and analytics projects (e.g., MMM, attribution, and other studies) into clear, actionable reporting.
   

  

**Requirements**
**Skills and Experience**
* Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent experience) in Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field
* Experience building and maintaining Power BI solutions, both independently and together with others
* Good knowledge of SQL
* Experience working in a cloud environment, preferably Google Cloud/BigQuery
* Understanding of how data flows and data models are set up to support stable reporting
* Swedish: good understanding in speech and writing
* English: fluent in speech and writing


**Meriting**
* Experience with data modeling and data integration
* Knowledge of DAXs and/or Python
* Experience from media/marketing environments (e.g., agency, publisher, platform)
* Knowledge of digital marketing and media measurement
* Experience working with or visualising outputs from measurement projects (e.g., MMM, attribution)


**Life At WPP Media & Benefits**
 Our passion for shaping the next era of media includes investing in our employees to help them do their best work, and we’re just as committed to employee growth as we are to responsible media investment. WPP Media employees can tap into the global WPP Media & WPP networks to pursue their passions, grow their networks, and learn at the cutting edge of marketing and advertising.
   

  

 In Sweden, our benefits include a SEK 3,000 wellness allowance, a 37,5-hour workweek, half-days and “klämdagar" off, 30 days of vacation, and two paid volunteer days per year. WPP Media Sweden is a member of Almega, and all employees are covered by a collective bargaining agreement (kollektivavtal), which ensures occupational pension, insurance, and other negotiated terms. We host regular team activities, knowledge-sharing sessions, and wellness initiatives throughout the year.
   

  

 WPP Media is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers. We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.
   

  

**Application**
 This is a full-time permanent position with a start date as soon as possible. We apply a six-month probationary period. In addition to reviewing your CV and conducting interviews, our recruitment process includes a testing phase. Selection and interviews are ongoing, so please submit your application with your CV and cover letter as soon as possible.
   

  

 We believe that diverse perspectives make us stronger and encourage applications from people of all genders, backgrounds, and experiences.
   

  

**Contact**
 If you have questions about the position, feel free to contact Viktor Åhlén, Head of Operations & Engineering viktor.ahlen@choreograph.com. Due to GDPR, we do not accept applications via email.
   

  

 Please read our Privacy Notice for more information on how we process the information you provide.

title: Software Support Engineer
description:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation, and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office-based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.
   

  

 The company is founder-led, profitable, and growing.
   

  

 We are hiring a Software Support Engineer to…
   

  

 …work in a dynamic and exciting engineering role in Linux-based infrastructure and applications, covering all layers of the stack, including bare metal, virtualization (KVM), containerization (Docker/LXC/LXD), storage (Ceph and Linux filesystems), networking (OVS, OVN and Core networking), OpenStack, Kubernetes and the open source applications running on top of them. It will challenge you to show the breadth of your engineering skills, which will be needed to work on almost any aspect of Ubuntu and the open source applications large enterprise customers run.
   

  

 This role is an opportunity for a technologist with a passion for Linux and open source to build a career with Canonical and drive success for our customers, community and the company. If you have an affinity for open source software, great communication skills, and a passion for troubleshooting and fixing issues in technology used by millions across the world, then you will enjoy working with some of the best people in the industry at Canonical.
   

  

 This role deals with critical issues in the open source stack that require upstream bug fixes. Our engineers are able to work productively at any level of the stack including the kernel and in a wide range of languages, to understand and address the software issues at hand. Our group is critical to the success of our customers, partners and Ubuntu itself.
   

  

 You will help with troubleshooting and driving issues to resolution with workarounds, guidance, and bug fixes to be released upstream and in Ubuntu.
   

  

**Location:** 
 This is a remote role, we have teams in all time zones.
   

  

 The role entails
   

  

* Resolve complex customer problems related to Ubuntu, Kernel, Ceph, OpenStack, or Kubernetes and other open source software
* Maintain a close working relationship with Canonical's Field, Support and product engineering teams
* Participate in upstream communities
* Develop bug fixes, backport patches, and work with upstream for inclusion
* Review code produced by other engineers
* Demonstrate good judgment in technical methods and techniques
* Prioritize work and manage your time effectively against those priorities
* Participate in team discussions to improve processes, tools, and documentation
* Maintain clear, technical and concise communications
* Work from home and travel internationally up to 10% of work time for team meetings, events and conferences


 What we are looking for in you
   

  

* An exceptional academic track record
* Background in Computer Science, STEM or similar
* Experience with Linux and open source software
* Experience with at least one of Python, Go, C or C++ on Linux
* A drive to learn unfamiliar technology and deep-dive difficult issues
* Willingness to travel up to 4 times a year for internal events


 Nice-to-have skills
   

  

* You love technology and working with brilliant people
* You are curious, flexible, articulate, and accountable
* You value soft skills and are passionate, enterprising, thoughtful, and self-motivated
* You have interest in, or willingness to learn about any of the following: Ubuntu Linux - kernel or userspace, Kubernetes, OpenStack, Ceph, QEMU/KVM, LXC/LXD,Postgresql, Mongo, Debian packaging, distributed systems


 What we offer colleagues
   

  

 We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognize outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
   

  

* Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Employee Assistance Programme & Wellness Platform
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
* Priority Pass and travel upgrades for long-haul company events


**About Canonical**
 Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open-source projects and the platform for AI, IoT, and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence; in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since our inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
   

  

 Canonical is an equal opportunity employer
   

  

 We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: Senior Frontend & UI Lead Developer
description:
Our client is developing a cutting-edge workshop ordering platform for the independent aftermarket. We are seeking a Senior Frontend & UI Lead who will oversee the interface end-to-end — from understanding workshop workflows to shipping production-ready code.
   

  

 This role requires a focus on user experience, thinking in user journeys before components, making design decisions directly in code, and presenting rationale confidently to both technical and business stakeholders.
   

  

 Main Responsibilities:
   

  

* Own the frontend architecture and overall user experience for the Used Parts platform.
* Translate complex workshop workflows into clear, efficient, and intuitive interfaces based on direct user needs.
* Design and ship production-grade UI, integrating design and implementation seamlessly.
* Collaborate with product, backend engineering, and business stakeholders to contribute actively to discussions.
* Use AI-assisted development workflows to enhance iteration speed and delivery output.
* Define, measure, and improve UX quality utilizing behavioral metrics.
* Facilitate cross-disciplinary decisions at the intersection of product, design, and engineering.


 Key Requirements:
   

  

* Significant experience as a Senior or Lead Frontend Engineer with ownership of user experience outcomes.
* Ability to move from user problem to deployed solution without reliance on a separate design handoff process.
* Strong capability in product, design, and engineering disciplines.
* Hands-on experience with AI-assisted development tools in daily workflows.
* Experience working in small, autonomous teams with collaboratively shaped strategic direction.
* Strong experience with React, Next.js, and TypeScript.


 Nice to Have:
   

  

* Experience with Tailwind CSS.
* Familiarity with shadcn/ui.
* Background in the automotive or vehicle industry.
* Experience in HMI or interface design for operational/workshop environments.
* Proven ability to communicate technical and design decisions to non-technical stakeholders.


 Other Details:
   

  

 Start: ASAP
   

  

 Length: Until 2026-06-30 with possibility of extension.
   

  

 Workplace: Central Stockholm

title: Senior Software Engineer - Content Experience Platform (JVM)
description:
***Welcome to Warner Bros. Discovery… the stuff dreams are made of.***








**Who We Are…**





 When we say, “the stuff dreams are made of,” we’re not just referring to the world of wizards, dragons and superheroes, or even to the wonders of Planet Earth. Behind WBD’s vast portfolio of iconic content and beloved brands, are the
 *storytellers* 
 bringing our characters to life, the
 *creators* 
 bringing them to your living rooms and the
 *dreamers* 
 creating what’s next…
 









 From brilliant creatives, to technology trailblazers, across the globe, WBD offers career defining opportunities, thoughtfully curated benefits, and the tools to explore and grow into your best selves. Here you are supported, here you are celebrated, here you can thrive.
 




  


 Warner Bros. has been entertaining audiences for more than 90 years through the world’s most-loved characters and franchises. Warner Bros. employs people all over the world in a wide variety of disciplines. We're always on the lookout for energetic, creative people to join our team.
 




  


**Your New Role...** 








 The Direct to Consumer (DTC) Group is a technology company within Warner Bros Discovery. We are building a global streaming video platform (OTT), and a suite of applications to support all of our network’s brands globally. We are building modern container-based micro-services operated on AWS. Our platform covers everything from search, content catalogue, video transcoding, personalization, to global subscriptions, and much more. We build for connected TVs, web, mobile phones, tablets, and consoles for a large footprint of WBD owned networks (Max, Discovery, Food Network, Golf TV, MotorTrend, Eurosport, Discovery Play, and many more). This is a growing, global engineering group crucial to WBD’s future. 
 







 We are hiring a Senior Software Engineer within the DTC Content Experience Platform. The team is focused on handling the metadata for the global content catalog. 
 







 We are responsible for indexing the metadata and implementing an API that makes it searchable for the different consumers. We ensure all client applications worldwide have seamless access to the latest metadata for various assets, including movies, series, and live sports events. 
 







 The ideal candidate:
 


* Has experience with high-performance, scalable systems that have been successfully shipped to customers in production.
* Drives best practices and sets standards for the team.
* Actively contributes to architectural discussions and team meetings.
* Shows good judgment making trade-offs between immediate and long-term business needs.
* Is an advocate for data-driven decision-making and is curious about solving complex technical challenges on a large scale.
* Takes ownership of their work and consistently delivers results in a fast-paced environment.



  


**Your Role Accountabilities...** 



* Lead the design and development of major functional changes to new or existing services owned by different teams.
* Troubleshoot a production issue to identify the root cause and, based on them, derive learnings to improve both development processes and system design.
* Provide guidance on design, coding, and operational best practices.
* Mentor junior engineers and positively influence the team.



  


**Qualifications and Experience...** 



* Proficient in Java or other JVM languages.
* Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and AWS.
* Deep understanding of distributed systems, micro-service-based architectures, and asynchronized communication (e.g., using gRPC and Kafka).
* Ability to implement alerting, metrics, and logging using tools like Prometheus, CloudWatch, Kibana, PagerDuty.
* Practical knowledge of persistence and caching solutions such as PostgreSQL, Redis, ElasticSearch, Caffeine.
* Familiar with asynchronous, non-blocking, functional/reactive programming styles such as Spring WebFlux or Vert.x.
* Experience designing high-performance, low-latency APIs for real time services.
* Experience with distributed tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry, Jaeger, Zipkin).
* Experience with GitOps, Terraform, ArgoCD, or Helm for infrastructure as code.



  


**​Additional Information**








 On December 5, Netflix and Warner Bros. Discovery announced they have entered into a definitive agreement under which Netflix will acquire Warner Bros., including its film and television studios, HBO Max and HBO. The transaction is expected to close after the separation of Warner Bros. Discovery's Global Networks division, Discovery Global, into a new publicly-traded company, which is expected to be completed in Q3 2026. For more details, including leadership appointments and information on individual brands, please visit our newsroom here.
 







 Although you will be hired by Warner Bros. Discovery, upon the planned spin-off of Discovery Global in 2026, your employment likely will transition to Warner Bros. During this period of transformation, you’ll have an exciting opportunity to lay the foundation at one of the world’s premier entertainment brands.
 















  


**How We Get Things Done…**









 This last bit is probably the most important! Here at WBD, our guiding principles are the core values by which we operate and are central to how we get things done. You can find them at
 












 www.wbd.com/guiding-principles/
 











  
 
 along with some insights from the team on what they mean and how they show up in their day to day. We hope they resonate with you and look forward to discussing them during your interview.
 








**Championing Inclusion at WBD**



 Warner Bros. Discovery embraces the opportunity to build a workforce that reflects a wide array of perspectives, backgrounds and experiences. Being an equal opportunity employer means that we take seriously our responsibility to consider qualified candidates on the basis of merit, regardless of sex, gender identity, ethnicity, age, sexual orientation, religion or belief, marital status, pregnancy, parenthood, disability or any other category protected by law.
 









 If you’re a qualified candidate with a disability and you require adjustments or accommodations during the job application and/or recruitment process, please visit our
 
 accessibility page
 
 for instructions to submit your request.

title: Principal Engineer -Public Edge Infrastructure
description:
**What You Will Do**
* Work as a hands-on Principal Engineer and own Klarna’s public edge architecture — the global entrypoint for all customer and partner traffic.
* Design and build the next-generation edge platform that public services rely on for secure, reliable, low-latency traffic handling.
* Define how services are exposed, secured, routed, and monitored at the edge — and implement the core patterns in production code.
* Design and implement the technical foundation for migrating 500+ production public endpoints to a scalable and secure model. For example: building reusable patterns so services can be made globally available with minimal custom configuration.
* Own and evolve edge security using AWS CloudFront and AWS WAF, defining secure-by-default patterns aligned with OWASP principles. For example: bot mitigation, rate limiting, and protection against common web attacks while keeping latency low.
* Simplify traffic flows and reduce architectural complexity at scale. For example: enabling more direct edge-to-service communication paths.
* Design and implement clear observability patterns for public infrastructure. For example: making it easy to understand how public entrypoints behave and how they relate to backend services.
* Build AI-assisted developer tooling that embeds edge standards directly into workflows. For example: using coding agents and reusable Claude-based skills to help teams migrate endpoints safely.
* Lead through engineering — write production code, ship core components, and set standards through working implementations.


**Who You Are**
* A deeply hands-on expert in public or edge infrastructure with strong experience in AWS CloudFront and AWS Web Application Firewall (WAF).
* You have personally designed and built large-scale, customer-facing systems operating at significant traffic levels.
* You have led architectural change by building reference implementations and enabling others to follow.
* You have strong TypeScript mastery and write production-grade infrastructure code and tooling in TypeScript (AWS CDK), with high standards for abstraction design and maintainability.
* You understand global traffic management, caching, TLS, routing, distributed system failure modes, and performance trade-offs.
* You have strong knowledge of web security fundamentals (e.g., OWASP Top 10) and experience applying edge protections such as bot mitigation and rate limiting.
* You are comfortable making architectural decisions with long-term impact and balancing security, performance, cost, and developer experience.
* You enjoy building systems yourself and reducing complexity through well-designed abstractions.


**Awesome to have**
* Experience with frontend engineering and modern frameworks such as React, especially in performance-sensitive environments.
* Strong knowledge of web performance optimization, including caching strategies and improving time-to-first-byte.
* Experience with edge execution or serverless request-handling models.
* Experience building AI-assisted developer tooling or automation systems.
* Experience defining infrastructure patterns adopted across multiple teams.
* Experience with other large-scale CDN or edge platforms such as Cloudflare, Fastly, or Akamai.Please include a CV in English.


 Curious to learn more about Klarna and what it’s like to work here? Explore our career site!

title: Senior Security Engineer (Application Security)
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The Role**
 At Legora, we prioritise security. Always.
   

  

 We are looking for Application Security Engineers who can think like an attacker, build like a developer, and operate like an SRE. In this role you will work across our whole tech stack, from our Azure cloud infrastructure, JavaScript and Python services, to our AI integrations and workflows. Your goal is to ensure everything we ship and build is secure-by-default and resilient to evolving threats.
   

  

 At Legora we don't believe that “shifting left” is sufficient, our goal is to build a secure and resilient ecosystem for our engineers so that they can build tomorrows features with confidence and speed.
   

  

* This is a Stockholm-based, 5-day in-office role, we believe building together in person drives better outcomes.


**What You Will Be Doing**
* Embed security into our software development lifecycle; conduct design reviews, threat modelling, and secure code reviews for our JavaScript and Python codebases.
* Architect and implement security controls for our cloud infrastructure, ensuring Zero Trust principles in across our networks, identities, and service-to-service communication.
* Build and maintain secure-by-default tooling, templates, and guardrails for our developers.
* Ensure our AI workflows and pipelines are secured and resilient against prompt injection, data leakage, and abuse.
* Develop and maintain vulnerability management pipelines, triaging and driving fixes with engineering teams.
* Automate security processes in CI/CD, including secret scanning, artifact signing, and policy-as-code checks.
* Partner with our information security team to ensure compliance and automate evidence collection efforts.


**Who You Are**
* You have 7+ years in Software Engineering with a strong interest in Cyber Security, and/or
* 10+ years as in Security Engineering or Product Security roles, ideally in a cloud-first, high-growth tech environment.
* You are able to produce production grade code.
* You have experience building and securing AI workflows, with an interest in adversarial AI threats and mitigations.
* You have hands-on experience securing cloud environments (Azure strongly preferred) and working with Infrastructure-as-Code (Pulumi, Terraform, or similar).
* You understand Zero Trust architecture and can design systems with identity-centric, least-privilege access controls.
* You have experience with secure SDLC practices, application security testing, and vulnerability management.
* You thrive in cross-functional work, explaining security risks to engineers, influencing design choices, and collaborating to deliver secure features on time.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Lösningsarkitekt för Kundkanaler (webb/app) och dess plattform till Ving
description:
**Vi vill ta nästa kliv i att göra våra webbar och appar ännu mer flexibla, lättrörliga och bättre för våra kunder. Därför söker vi nu en lösningsarkitekt som vill vara med och forma framtidens digitala kundmöten tillsammans med oss.**
**Om tjänsten**
 Vi söker en lösningsarkitekt som vill vara med och driva utvecklingen av våra webb- och appkanaler i en modern microfrontend-arkitektur. Här får du en nyckelroll i att skapa lösningar som är skalbara, användarvänliga och sömlöst integrerade med våra backend-system.
   

  

 Vi arbetar hyptes- och datadrivet, där vi lär oss och justerar kontinuerligt. Du kommer att tillhöra ett av våra kundreseplattformsteam - Team Fundamentals - men kommer jobba teamöverskridande med att skapa lösningar som optimerar hela flöden.
   

  

 Som lösningsarkitekt hos oss kommer du att:
   

  

  

* Ta ansvar för arkitektur och design av våra webbaserade och mobila lösningar.
* Leda teamen i att förstå och omvandla verksamhetsbehov till lösningar
* Arbeta nära våra frontendteam och stötta dem i val av ramverk, designmönster och tekniska beslut.
* Säkerställa en smidig integration mellan frontend och backend genom väldefinierade API:er och integrationsmönster.
* Delta i tekniska diskussioner och vara en brygga mellan utvecklare, produktägare och andra arkitekter.


 Vår försäljning sker primärt i våra appar/webbar - det du bygger påverkar direkt vår kundupplevelse! Hos oss jobbar vi agilt i snabba releasecykler och med continuous delivery . Teamen ansvarar för hela kedjan från kravförståelse, utveckling, deploy till förvaltning och support. Vår strategi är ”Cloud first” varför vi värdesätter kunskap om molnlösningar och Azure.
   

  

**Vår tekniska miljö**
 Vi är 13 utvecklingsteam på IT-avdelningen i Stockholm som tillsammans driver utvecklingen framåt. Våra system byggs i en modern Microsoft-miljö och vi arbetar med aktuella tekniker, ramverk och verktyg som .NET Core, C#, Node, Azure, React, React Native, JavaScript, GraphQL, MS-SQL och Redis.
   

  

 I linje med vår cloud-first-strategi satsar vi på skalbara och framtidssäkra lösningar där innovation, prestanda och kvalitet alltid står i fokus.
   

  

 Läs mer om
 **vår IT-avdelning här** 
 🧑‍💻
   

  

****Vem vi söker****
**Kvalifikationer:**
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet som lösningsarkitekt för moderna ecom lösningar.
* Erfarenhet av/förmågan att dokumentera och kommunicera beslut, principer etc
* Hålla workshops för att ta fram lösnings-design
* Gedigen erfarenhet av frontendutveckling (t.ex. React).
* Stark förståelse för microfrontend-principer och hur de implementeras i större organisationer.
* God kunskap om API-design, integrationer och backendkommunikation.
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta i agila team och med DevOps-principer.
* Förmåga att se helheten och samtidigt dyka ner i detaljer när det behövs.
* Relevant högskole- eller universitetsutbildning
* Flytande svenska i tal och skrift


**Meriterande**
* Intresse för generativ AI och förmåga att driva frågor inom området
* Erfarenhet av molnplattformar (Azure).
* Kunskap om CI/CD pipelines och containerteknik (t.ex. Docker, Kubernetes).
* Kunskap i att bygga säkra web- och appplattformar.


 Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper. Som person är du kommunikativ och trivs med att samarbeta i tvärfunktionella team. Du har lätt för att förklara och förankra tekniska lösningar för både utvecklare och icke-tekniska intressenter. Du gillar att utmana gamla arbetssätt och föreslå nya idéer, och du motiveras av att se dina lösningar göra skillnad för både användare och verksamhet.
   

  

 Du har en gedigen teknisk kunskap men en lika stor insikt i hur man leder för att skörda styrkan i samarbete för att nå gemensamma mål. Med ett proaktivt förhållningssätt och förmågan att se helheten bidrar du till att våra lösningar blir optimerade för både användare, verksamhet och IT.
   

  

 Du är en lagspelare som gärna delar med dig av din kunskap, coachar kollegor och bidrar till en öppen och lärande kultur.
   

  

****Vi erbjuder****
 Vi erbjuder en spännande och utvecklande miljö där du får möjlighet att utvecklas och påverka. Du får en omväxlande vardag tillsammans med härliga kollegor - vi arbetar i nära samarbete med andra team och verksamheter för att ge våra gäster de bästa veckorna på året. Vi har dessutom ett högt medarbetarengagemang som år efter år visar sig i vår interna medarbetarundersökning. Vi älskar vårt jobb och välkomnar dig ombord på vår resa i Nordic Leisure Travel Group!
   

  

 Läs mer om
 **vad vi erbjuder här** 
 🏄
   

  

****Övrigt****
 Tjänsten är en heltidstjänst och placerad på Vings fina huvudkontor på Kungsholmen i Stockholm. Det är möjligt med distansarbete upp till 2 dagar per vecka när det passar för teamet/arbetsuppgiften.
   

  

 Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!
   

  

**I denna rekrytering använder vi oss av tester från Alva Labs – ett logiskt problemlösningstest och en personlighetsanalys. Testerna utgår från vedertagen forskning och ger oss möjlighet att på ett mer objektivt sätt bedöma dina styrkor och eventuella utmaningar i relation till aktuell tjänst. Du får mer information när du skickat in din ansökan, men om du är nyfiken redan nu kan du läsa mer hos** 
**Alva Labs** 
**.**
**Vi ställer höga krav på våra medarbetares säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetenhet. Innan ett beslut om anställning fattas kommer en bakgrundskontroll att genomföras.**
**🏄‍♂️ Kul på jobbet**
**🤝 Tjänstepension**
**🌎 Reseförmåner**
**🦉 Utveckling och lärande**
**💗 Well-being**
**💼 Hybrid arbetsplats**
 Läs mer om vad vi erbjuder här
   

  

 Ving ingår i den nordiska resekoncernenNordic Leisure Travel Group.
   

  

 I gruppen ingår researrangörerna Ving Sverige, Ving Norge, Globetrotter, Spies i Danmark, Tjäreborg i Finland, flygbolaget Sunclass Airlines samt våra egna hotellkoncept Sunwing Family Resorts, Ocean Beach Club och Sunprime Hotels. Gruppen säljer ett normalt år runt 1 250 000 resor i de nordiska länderna.

title: Senior Data Engineer – Stockholm
description:
Är du en erfaren Dataingenjör, BI-utvecklare/BI-konsult eller ETL-utvecklare som vill ta nästa steg i din karriär? Drömmer du om att arbeta med modern teknik och lösa komplexa problem tillsammans med ett nätverk av skickliga Data- och Analysexperter? Då kan du vara den vi söker!
   

  

 Våra närmare 100 dataexperter i Sverige jobbar med datalösningar inom nästan alla teknologier. Ett vanligt projekt varierar i längd från en månad till några år, och situationen kan justeras baserat på dina önskemål. Våra kunder finns inom alla branscher, allt från produktutveckling, hälsa, industri och försäkring till kommunikation, bank och elförsörjning.
   

  

**Möt en av våra Data Engineers:** 
 Clara Lundström arbetar i ett projekt för ett globalt företag inom facility service, det största i Norden. Tillsammans med sitt team illustrerar hon datalandskapet och använder data från olika källor för att skapa visualiseringar. Clara beskriver arbetet på Solita som varierat och utvecklande, med tillgång till ett stort nätverk av experter inom många olika områden.
   

  

**Vad Vi Erbjuder**
* En företagskultur som präglas av autonomi, stöd och gemensam tillväxt.
* Spännande kundprojekt och erfarna kollegor som är omtänksamma och passionerade.
* Möjlighet att arbeta med en stor bredd av projekt och branscher.
* Partnerskap med ledande tech-företag som GCP, AWS och Snowflake.


**Känner du igen dig i följande?**
 Som Data Engineer på Solita jobbar du brett; end-to-end från ingestion hela vägen till visualisering. Det är bra om du har erfarenhet inom; pipelines, ingestion, datawarehousing modellering, analys och visualisering. Exempel på tekniker vi arbetar med är Snowflake, Matillion, DBT, Databricks, Fabric, Data factory, Bigquery och mer! Vi jobbar även ofta i cloud och arbetar primärt i Python eller SQL. Som konsult på Solita är det viktigt att du kan kommunicera tekniska lösningar till kunder och hantera deras behov och problem. Du behärskar även svenska och engelska flytande.
   

  

**Dina Ansvarsområden i Rollen**
* Självständigt leverera komplexa uppdrag inom ditt expertområde och planera de bästa åtgärderna för att nå önskade resultat.
* Utveckla arbetsmetoder och processer samt vägleda och agera mentor till andra.
* Analysera nya problem, bedöma teknisk information och andra källor, samt utveckla nya lösningar.
* Optimera befintliga processer och delta i utvecklingen av nya processer av avdelningsövergripande betydelse.
* Leverera lösningar till kunder och samarbeta nära med dem för att förstå och möta deras behov.


 Det viktigaste för oss är ändå att du delar våra värderingar, du är omtänksam mot andra och delar gärna din kunskap och vågar stå för det du tror på. Vår företagskultur är väldigt viktig för oss, och vi värdesätter låg hierarki, självständighet och ansvar, värdeskapande arbete, och trevliga kollegor. Här kan du läsa mer om vår kultur, och här kan du bläddra bland våra tech-bloggar.
   

  

**En dag på Solita:** 
 Din dag kan börja med ett standup-möte med kund och team-medlemmar, följt av kodnära arbete som att bygga data pipelines och programmera i Python/SQL. Under dagen kan det dyka upp ad-hoc möten med kund eller kollegor, eller interna utbildningsmöten. Dagen avslutas ofta med mer kodarbete, möten och inte minst – fika!
   

  

**Våra förmåner**
 Hos Oss Får Du Inte Bara Trevliga Kollegor Utan Du Får Även Ta Del Av Ett Brett Förmånspaket. Här Är Några Utav Våra Förmåner
   

  

 💰 Fast lön, oberoende av beläggningsgrad
   

  

 🏖️ 30 semesterdagar
   

  

 ⚖️ Interna mentorskapsprogram
   

  

 🛋️ Möjligheter till flex för en god work-life-balance
   

  

 🪩 After Work och hobbyaktiviteter
   

  

 🔗 Intern coach för att stödja ditt välbefinnande
   

  

 🥐 Fredagsfrukost
   

  

**Intresserad?**
 Vid frågor; kontakta jacob.beck(at)solita.se eller ansök här. Du kan också connecta med Jacob på LinkedIn på https://www.linkedin.com/in/jacob-beck-/. Vi uppskattar dina färdigheter, men vi är också ivriga att lära oss om essensen i din motivation och hur du ser din tillväxt framför dig. Dela ditt CV nedan så återkommer vi så fort vi kan.
   

  

 Sign up to our newsletter to hear about the latest news, upcoming events and open positions at Solita
   

  

 Subscribe
   

  

 Solita is an AI and data transformation company that empowers businesses and societies to reinvent themselves. The company combines advanced technology, data innovation, and human insight to deliver strategic consulting, service design, software development, AI and analytics, and managed cloud services. Founded in 1996, Solita has grown into a vibrant community of more than 2,100 forward-thinkers, operating across nine countries: Finland, Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Belgium, Poland, Switzerland, Germany, and the UK.
   

  

 At Solita, we are committed to equality, diversity, and non-discrimination. We aim to create a workplace where everyone feels welcome, valued and respected. In our recruitment processes, we evaluate candidates based on competence, skills and potential.

title: Senior Product Manager – Energy Intelligence (12 Month Contract)
description:
10% of Europe's CO₂ emissions are caused by residential heating, and energy bills are out of control. Aira is here to change that.
   

  

 With our intelligent Aira Heat Pump at the heart, we're making the switch to clean energy-tech easier and more affordable. Helping Europe turn off gas and slashing energy costs one home at a time.
   

  

 Founded in 2022, and with 1000+ employees and counting, Aira is driving the clean energy revolution by providing homes with modern clean energy-tech solutions such as solar power, battery storage solutions, and heat pumps. With a state-of-the-art production facility in Poland, an HQ in Stockholm, Sweden, and hubs across the UK, Germany, and Italy, Aira is well on track to install clean energy-tech into 5 million homes by 2034.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to lead the strategy, roadmap, and execution of our next-generation
 **Energy Intelligence** 
 platform. This role sits at the intersection of user experience and AI across our home energy system – heat pump, inverter, battery and solar. You will shape the direction to ensure our intelligence delivers real, scalable customer value.
   

  

 This is an opportunity for a product leader who thrives on strategic thinking, technical depth, and complex cross-team collaboration.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Own the product strategy, priorities and OKRs for Energy optimisation and Aira Intelligence.
* Turn customer insights, strategic goals, and market trends into clear and actionable product direction.
* Build a deep understanding of the emerging energy and flexibility markets in Europe
* Collaborate closely with UX-, App-, IoT- and Connectivity- teams to deliver high-impact intelligence features.
* Lead and manage external partner relationships and integration roadmaps.
* Guide your team in problem framing, success metrics, and customer value. As well as trade-offs, prioritization, and complex technical decision-making.
* Champion and evangelize Intelligence features across internal and external communication.


 We're transforming how clean energy solutions are designed and brought to customers across Europe. If you're motivated by solving challenging product problems in a mission-driven, high-growth environment, this role offers real impact and visibility.
   

  

**What You Bring To The Team**
* Proven experience in product management
* Proven experience within energy flexmarket and/or ML/AI
* Strong strategic judgment with the ability to balance customer needs, business priorities, and technical constraints.
* Ability to go discuss in depth with technical teams — models, optimisation logic, data flows, architecture
* Experience working with cross-functional teams and external partners.
* Excellent communication and an ability to bring clarity to complex domains.


**Bonus Experience** 
 (any combination of these is valuable):
   

  

  

* High-growth, scaling environments with rapid market expansion
* Background in energy markets, VPPs, flexibility services, demand response, or energy trading.


**What is in it for you?**
* Gain hands-on experience in a fast-growing scale-up, with a supportive and inclusive culture, and career advancement opportunities.
* Collaborate with diverse teams across international markets and launch products globally.
* Develop and grow personally and professionally in an entrepreneurial-minded environment.
* Thrive in a dynamic, solution-oriented culture, tackling challenges and driving meaningful solutions that make a positive impact on the world.
* Influence the company's success, with a visible impact on product development and market growth.
* Enjoy the flexibility of our hybrid work model and discover our brand-new office at Norra Stationsgatan 93 in Stockholm. Conveniently located near public transport, with Sankt Eriksplan and Odenplan stations just a few minutes' walk away


 At Aira, we champion the next-generation energy as well as diversity and inclusion. We firmly believe that a diverse workforce fosters innovation and creativity, enhancing our ability to serve customers and communities effectively. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, cultures, and perspectives to join our team. Our commitment is to uphold equality and inclusivity, fostering a collaborative environment where everyone can thrive and contribute to a better future for our people and the planet.

title: Senior Frontend & UI Lead Developer

 Start: ASAP
   

  

 Length: Until 2026-06-30 with possibility of extension.
   

  

 Workplace: Central Stockholm

title: Strategic Technology Executive  Europe
description:
**What You’ll Do**
 The Strategic Technology Executive role at Teradata is a strategic, customer-facing executive position responsible for communicating Teradata’s product vision and platform differentiation directly to senior customer stakeholders. Reporting into the WW VantageCloud Platform Architecture organization, this role serves as a unified global voice from the field into Product, while also delivering consistent strategic messaging to customers.
   

  

 The Field CTO is a technologist, market analyst, and trusted advisor who influences Teradata’s long-term product strategy through close customer collaboration and participation in cross-functional forums. This role will be a part of the Global Field CTO function within the Platform Architecture team enabling a tighter feedback loop from the field to Product and ensuring customer requirement alignment across regions and industries.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
 Voice to Customers (Outbound Focus)
   

  

* Act as Teradata’s technical executive liaison to customer C-levels and senior leadership, articulating our long-term product vision and competitive differentiation.
* Lead strategic discussions on cloud modernization, hybrid/cloud architectures, and the Vantage roadmap (SQL to AI/ML to GenAI).
* Deliver executive briefings, workshops, and thought leadership at public conferences and customer events.
* Guide Solution Engineers (SEs) and Account Teams in strategic technical messaging and positioning.
* Drive consistency in outbound messaging through reusable assets, “gold standard” decks, white papers, and social content.


 Voice into Product (Inbound Focus)
   

  

* Represent global field and customer perspectives in product strategy, roadmap planning, and prioritization sessions.
* Regularly interface with Product Management, Engineering, Product Marketing, and the Office of the Strategic Technology Executives
* Contribute to strategic forums including Product Advisory Councils, POC feedback loops, and roadmap workshops.
* Define and communicate long-range customer-driven needs (e. g. , 2–4 year vision and North Star alignment).
* Partner with Product Marketing to refine customer-facing messaging based on field experience.


 Global Leadership & Coordination
   

  

* Participate in a unified, global Strategic Technology Executive function that ensures consistent feedback loops and messaging across all geographies and industries.
* Coordinate and contribute to global initiatives such as internal knowledge sharing, bi-weekly Strategic Technology Executive alignment meetings, and consolidated product feedback summaries.
* Contribute to standing up the global Strategic Technology Executive operating model, including selection of regional representatives to support requirement gathering and formalizing engagement mechanisms with Product.


**What Makes a Qualified Candidate**
* Proven record of 15+ years of technical sales leadership, field technology advocacy, or product strategy roles.
* Deep technical knowledge in cloud architecture (AWS, Azure, GCP), data platforms, data architecture, hybrid environments, and AI/ML use cases.
* Executive presence and the ability to communicate complex topics clearly to both technical and business audiences.
* Experience directly influencing product strategy through clearly structured feedback mechanisms.
* Ability to collaborate across engineering, marketing, and sales organizations to drive unified strategy execution.
* Demonstrated thought leadership via public speaking, publications, or recognized contributions to the analytics/cloud space.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Technical degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (Master’s or MBA is a plus).
* Prior experience in global, matrixed organizations with customer-facing strategic roles.
* Public cloud certifications (e. g. , AWS Solutions Architect, Azure Expert) preferred.
* Demonstrated ability to drive customer digital transformation and business outcomes through data and analytics.


**What You’ll Get**
* The opportunity to help shape Teradata’s future product vision.
* A platform to drive innovation and elevate customer conversations.
* The ability to build a globally influential network and be a change agent at the intersection of technology and business.


**Why We Think You’ll Love Teradata**
 We prioritize a people-first culture because we know our people are at the very heart of our success. We embrace a flexible work model because we trust our people to make decisions about how, when, and where they work. We focus on well-being because we care about our people and their ability to thrive both personally and professionally. We are an anti-racist company because our dedication to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion is more than a statement. It is a deep commitment to doing the work to foster an equitable environment that celebrates people for all of who they are.
   

  

 Teradata invites all identities and backgrounds in the workplace. We work with deliberation and intent to ensure we are cultivating collaboration and inclusivity across our global organization. ​ We are proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, color, ancestry, religion, creed, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, or related conditions), national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, gender identity or expression, military and veteran status, or any other legally protected status.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
**Who You'll Work With**
 You are someone who thrives in a high-performance environment, bringing a growth mindset and entrepreneurial spirit to tackle meaningful challenges that have a real impact.
   

  

 In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we’ll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities to help you quickly broaden your expertise, grow into a well-rounded professional, and contribute to work that truly makes a difference.
   

  

 When you join us, you will have:
   

  

* Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
* A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
* Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
* Exceptional benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.


**Your Impact**
 You will work closely with 1-2 of our Senior Partners, providing dedicated support, proactively organizing and managing their complex workday and creating meaningful external relations.
   

  

 You will be in daily contact with national and international colleagues and clients, as well as planning internal and external events. You will work in a warm, caring, and fun work atmosphere in the center of Stockholm with highly motivated professionals as your colleagues.
   

  

 You will become part of a team of professional and dedicated Executive Assistants, who daily daily support each other through knowledge sharing, giving a helping hand when needed, and cover as backup for each other.
   

  

 You will proactively manage your Partner’s calendar and travel arrangements, provide broad administrative support, manage incoming communications, and prioritize emails to help organize and leverage time effectively as well as driving ad hoc tasks of various size and complexity. Moreover, you will assist in preparing presentations and materials, ensuring that the material is complete and accurate. Therefore, it is a prerequisite that you have excellent communication skills in both English and Swedish.
   

  

 As a person you are proactive, entrepreneurial, and resilient with a professional can-do attitude and always willing to go the extra mile. You find it exciting, challenging, and rewarding to successfully solve problems as part of your everyday job. You remain service-minded in your approach, exhibiting a high level of professionalism and exercise discretion.
   

  

**Your Qualifications and Skills**
* +2 years of experience as an Executive Assistant or from a similar fast-paced and high standard environment
* Customer-oriented mindset and approach: your focus is on how to best support our Partners, clients and colleagues
* Entrepreneurial, highly motivated, have great organizational skills, and you enjoy solving calendar scheduling as well as other administrative tasks
* Reliable, flexible and adaptable to changes, detail-oriented and can handle expenses and timesheets with accuracy and care
* Enjoys working in a dynamic environment with caring colleagues
* Proactive self-starter with a positive can-do attitude
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
* Efficient in driving improvements and embracing challenges as opportunities for development
* Embodies McKinsey culture, adheres to firm policies, and acts as a role model
* Excellent written and spoken communication skills in English and Swedish at native level

title: Head of CRM
description:
Do you want to help millions of anglers achieve their fishing dreams? At
 **Fishbrain** 
 , we’re on a mission to deliver an incredible experience for everyone who loves fishing and the outdoors, today and tomorrow.
   

  

 Fishbrain is the world’s most popular fishing app, used by millions of anglers in the US and beyond. CRM is a central part of how we build long-lasting relationships with our users and guide them through every step of their journey. Our lifecycle communication has a direct impact on activation, retention, and subscription growth and we are now looking for a hands-on leader to run this engine.
   

  

 You will report to the CMO, who defines the broader commercial strategy, and you are the one who brings it to life. As
 **Head of CRM** 
 you'll take the helm of our lifecycle efforts. In this role, you’ll lead a talented team through a constant cycle of execution, optimization, and experimentation to help our community thrive. You are a "player-coach" who enjoys the balance between high-level roadmap planning and the day-to-day work inside our tools.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Lead, coach, and support a team of CRM Managers, while actively participating in the day-to-day execution.
* Be responsible for the performance of our lifecycle flows (activation, retention, and monetization) and ensure they align with our commercial goals.
* Partner closely with product teams and collaborate with the wider marketing team to create a cohesive user journey that supports acquisition, engagement and long-term retention.
* Ensure all customer communication is helpful, valuable and aligned with in-app experiences and product developments.
* Use product updates, feature launches and seasonal opportunities to create relevant customer communication that supports the overall user experience.
* Design, launch and improve automated and campaign-based lifecycle flows.
* Work hands-on in Braze to build segmented, personalized, and event-driven journeys.
* Run A/B tests and experiments to find the best cadence and messaging. You don’t just report data, you turn it into immediate action.


**What We’re Looking For**
* 5+ years of experience in CRM, marketing automation, lifecycle or retention marketing within a B2C digital or subscription-based business.
* You are comfortable working directly in tools like Braze or similar platforms. You enjoy the craft of building flows as much as the logic behind them.
* Proven ability to design and execute lifecycle and CRM initiatives that drive measurable business results.
* Strong understanding of user segmentation, behavioral targeting and multi-channel communication.
* Experience leading teams and working closely with product and cross-functional stakeholders.
* A collaborative leader who enjoys solving customer problems and improving the user journey.
* Strong analytical skills and the ability to work confidently with data to guide decisions and improvements.
* You are comfortable taking a lead role in projects, ensuring clarity and follow-through for your team.
* Fluent in English, both written and spoken.


**Nice to have**
* Experience working in a tech scale-up environment.
* An interest in the outdoors or fishing, it helps you understand the "vibe" and needs of our users.


**Where you’ll work**
 We work in a
 **hybrid setup** 
 , and all team members are expected to work from our Stockholm HQ, The Lighthouse, at least two days per week.
   

  

 Please submit applications in
 **ENGLISH** 
 , those that are not will not be reviewed.
   

  

**Our Bait**
 We’re a product-focused startup, and every individual is important. That means you get to contribute to the direction of the company and the impact we have on millions of anglers. We at Fishbrain work with the latest tech and challenge ourselves every day. We have a work culture that encourages innovation, passion and teamwork. And most importantly, we want you to enjoy coming to work every single day.
   

  

**In Addition, Fishbrain Also Offers**
 🌴 30 days annual vacation
   

  

 Each year you’ll get 30 days vacation to use however, whenever
   

  

 🤸‍♀️ Wellness bonus
   

  

 5000 sek annual allowance to put towards anything in the realm of health and fitness
   

  

 🧑‍🎓 Learning & development days
   

  

 Fishbrain provides five paid Learning & Development days per year to its employees
   

  

 💻📱 Technology of your choice
   

  

 Mac or PC, you decide
   

  

 🌎 Commitment to sustainability
   

  

 Our global data set, among the largest in the world, is selectively provided in a managed and controlled manner to scientific research partners in both North America and Europe to better understand and improve fisheries research
   

  

 🤧🫷Flu vaccination
   

  

 To support your health and well-being, we’ll be offering complimentary flu vaccinations every year
   

  

 🎣 Fishing rods in the office
   

  

 Grab one and go fishing. We have fishing guides and experts working among us that can help you catch your dream fish
   

  

 🐕 Dog friendly
   

  

 Feel free to bring your furry friend to the office
   

  

 🥳 Social activities
   

  

 Quizzes, virtual escape rooms, parties, regular fikas, fishing tournaments and much more!
   

  

 🥪 Breakfast
   

  

 Every Wednesday we start the day with a breakfast together
   

  

 🍭 Candy, soda, fruits & snacks☕️ Coffee and tea🎮 Gaming corner🏓 Ping pong
   

  

**Feeling hooked? Throw us a line!**

title: INTERNSHIP - PR & film release strategy
description:
**Rio Releases Film with a focus on Nordic audiences aged 18–35** 




  





 As an intern at Rio, you’ll step straight into the PR engine behind our film releases – from early positioning and first buzz to premiere week and beyond. You’ll work closely with the team to craft communication strategies, find the sharpest angles for each title, and help shape how our films show up in the world – across press, partnerships and digital culture.
